steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment.
Select representative measurement points or paths for monitoring.
Ensure measurement locations provide data that will meet RA requirements.
Calculate the equivalent duct diameter as per 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Section 2.1.
Install the CEMS and prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift and zero drift once each day for 7 consecutive days while the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load.
Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day for 7 consecutive days.
Conduct a RA test following the CD test period.
Conduct the RA test according to the procedure given in Section 7.
Verify the ability of the CEMS to conform to the established CEMS calibration used for determining the emission concentration.
Conduct the CD test immediately before any adjustments to the CEMS zero and calibration settings, or in such a way that the CD and ZD can be determined.
Conduct the CD and ZD tests at the points specified in Sections 4.3 and 4.4.
Calculate the calibration drift of the CEMS in percent.
Calculate the zero drift of the CEMS in percent.
Conduct RM measurements to verify the initial performance of the entire CEMS system.
Ensure a measure of relative accuracy at a single level for each metal measured for compliance purposes by the CEMS.
Note the beginning and end of each RM test period of each run in the CEMS data log.
Correlate the CEMS and RM test data as to the time and duration by determining from the CEMS final output the integrated average pollutant concentration for each RM test period.
Confirm that the pair of results are on a consistent moisture, temperature, and diluent concentration basis.
Compare each integrated CEMS value against the corresponding average RM value.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements for each metal required and at each level required.

Ensure that if more than nine pairs of measurements are obtained, up to three pairs of measurements may be rejected so long as the total number of measurement pairs used to determine the RA is greater than or equal to nine.
Summarize the results on a data sheet.

preconditions.text: If the cause of failure to meet the RA test is determined to be the measurement location and a satisfactory correction technique cannot be established, the Administrator may require the CEMS to be relocated.
Before determining the CD and ZD, ensure the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load.
Before conducting a RA test, ensure the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load.
Before conducting the CD test, ensure that no unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place during the prior 24-hour period.
If iron is chosen for monitoring, ensure the three levels correspond to those for one of the metals.
